auth_module_map.role: Core auth models: AbstractBaseUser, AbstractUser, User, UserManager; password/last_login infrastructure
ModelBackend authentication handler; authenticate() method signature determines field support
Auth app configuration; ready() hook for signal handling and custom user model setup

migration_trace: 1. Analyze AbstractBaseUser (django/contrib/auth/models.py:~80-150): provides password hashing, last_login DateTimeField, is_active BooleanField
3. Identify field mappings: username→username_field, email→email_field, password→password_field, last_login→last_login (built-in only); profile_tier/external_sso_id/last_sync_at are unmappable
4. Review UserManager (django/contrib/auth/managers.py): create_user() creates instance with USERNAME_FIELD; create_superuser() sets is_staff/is_superuser
5. Review ModelBackend (django/contrib/auth/backends.py:authenticate method): accepts only username+password; does not support external_sso_id or custom tier checks

6. Strategy: Create custom User model extending AbstractBaseUser; define all three unmappable fields; create custom UserManager overriding create_user/create_superuser; implement custom backend for SSO auth

unmappable_fields.reason: No equivalent subscription/tier field in AbstractBaseUser or AbstractUser; tier management is application-specific business logic outside auth framework scope
unmappable_fields.migration_strategy: Add CharField(max_length=20, choices=[("free","Free"),("pro","Pro"),("enterprise","Enterprise")], default="free") as direct field on custom User model; manage tier changes through custom signals or middleware, not auth pipeline
unmappable_fields.evidence: django/contrib/auth/models.py:~150-220 where AbstractUser fields are defined (username, email, first_name, last_name, is_staff, is_active, is_superuser, date_joined, groups, user_permissions) - no tier equivalent exists

unmappable_fields.reason: SSO provider integration is external to Django contrib.auth; no built-in field for mapping to external identity systems; ModelBackend.authenticate() only accepts username/password
unmappable_fields.migration_strategy: Add CharField(max_length=255, unique=True, null=True, blank=True) to custom User model; create custom authentication backend (subclass of ModelBackend in django/contrib/auth/backends.py) that overrides authenticate() to accept and validate external_sso_id instead of/alongside password
unmappable_fields.evidence: django/contrib/auth/backends.py:ModelBackend.authenticate(request, username=None, password=None) method signature - only username/password parameters; no external_sso_id support; custom backends can override this method completely

unmappable_fields.reason: Application-specific sync tracking timestamp; differs from last_login which only updates on successful password authentication; no Django built-in for general-purpose sync tracking
unmappable_fields.migration_strategy: Add DateTimeField(null=True, blank=True) to custom User model; update via custom signals (post_save handlers) or middleware when sync occurs, not through standard auth flow; do not conflate with last_login field from AbstractBaseUser
unmappable_fields.evidence: django/contrib/auth/models.py:~100-110 where AbstractBaseUser.last_login is defined as DateTimeField(null=True) - only updated by auth backend on successful authentication; has no application-level sync semantics
